title: Understand: Elections and Voting: An Overview
description: Check your understanding of the elections and voting with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: Elections and Voting Rights: From the Founding through the Civil Rights Movement
Understand: Elections and Voting: An Overview

assessment_items.question_content:
**The original Constitution largely left elections and voting to \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**The Fifteenth Amendment passed in 1870 banned voting discrimination on the basis of what?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Although voting requirements varied state to state, which of the following were some requirements for voting that existed in the early years of America?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

**During the Age of Jackson in the 1820s and 1830s, some voting requirements were eliminated and it became much easier to vote if you were \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a white man, whether or not you owned property

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which section of the original Constitution established the Electoral College?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which statement best describes how the original Constitution delegated authority over elections and voting?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It left voting issues—like time, place, and manner of elections—primarily to the states.
It gave Congress overwhelming power to manage how elections were held.
It allowed states to control internal elections, but gave Congress power over federal elections.
It created an independent body called the Electoral College to oversee elections at all levels of government.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which amendment to the U. S. Constitution changed the Electoral College by requiring the electors to cast separate ballots for president and vice president?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The Seventeenth Amendment allowed for the direct election of U.S. senators. 

**This revised which part of the original Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**By 1840, which group of people generally secured the right to vote?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**By 1870, which groups of people generally secured the right to vote?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**By 1920, which groups of people had formally secured the right to vote?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which statement best describes how the Twenty-Sixth Amendment changed voting in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It provided for the popular election of the president.
It extended the right to vote to Native Americans.
It prohibited states from using poll taxes as a barrier to voting.
It lowered the voting age from 21 to 18.
